accept: act close runs the per-issue subset of doctor checks after writing the op
failed correlation warns (and either soft-fails or hard-fails — design call)
--no-doctor flag on close opts out of the check
payload.description: If commit-marker discipline is load-bearing (act doctor uses (act-XXXX) markers to correlate work commits with closed issues), close should verify it at close time rather than discovering mismatches at the next doctor run. Even just the single-issue subset — does the marker appear in the linked work commit? — would catch bare-id-slicing errors and other marker-construction bugs locally. --no-doctor flag opts out for trusted-fast paths. Source: docs/aac-website-dogfood-debrief.md §7.
payload.title: act doctor as part of close to verify commit-marker correlation
